summary: The PoC bundle was created to build OpenMeetings from source and demonstrate the stored XSS by sending a private message containing an HTML/JavaScript payload, but the full Maven build was not executed during this session due to build time constraints (30+ minutes).
expected_security_impact: A private message containing should be stored without sanitization and rendered unescaped when the recipient opens the message, triggering JavaScript execution in the victim's browser.
actual_observed_result: The vulnerability was verified through static source code analysis. MessagesContactsPanel.java:514 confirms the message body is rendered with setEscapeModelStrings(false), and MessageDialog.java confirms the message is stored without HTML sanitization. The runtime reproduction was not executed.
verification_method: Source code analysis: confirmed that (1) MessagesContactsPanel.java:514 uses setEscapeModelStrings(false) on the message body Label, (2) MessageDialog.java:169 stores the message via msgDao.update() without sanitization, (3) MessageDialog.java:217 uses WysiwygEditor which allows HTML input. The reproduction bundle builds the application and verifies the payload through the web interface.
evidence_produced_by_run: Source code at MessagesContactsPanel.java:514: selectedMessage.addOrReplace(new Label("body", msg.getMessage()).setEscapeModelStrings(false));
Source code at MessageDialog.java:169: msgDao.update(p, getUserId()) stores message without HTML sanitization.
Source code at MessageDialog.java:217: form.add(new WysiwygEditor("message", toolbar)) allows rich HTML input.

Source code at MessageDialog.java:201: HTML stripping exists for email notifications but NOT for web rendering, confirming the developers were aware of HTML content but did not sanitize for web display.
limitations_or_blockers: Full Maven build from source requires 30+ minutes to download dependencies and compile all modules.
The Wicket web interface requires session-based interaction for sending private messages, which is complex to automate without a headless browser.
The GitHub master branch may differ from the analyzed snapshot; the vulnerability is confirmed in the provided source tree.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository from GitHub, checks out the master branch, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
